name: Elliot(pre-forsaken)
description:
MAKE YOUR OWN SCENARIO

PROXY✅

I recommend using a proxy for my bots. JanitorLLM really sucks. I know my bots contain a lot of tokens, and JLLM isn't suitable for that; it will work poorly, get confused, and forget. So, I recommend using a proxy.

If the bot is writing for you, I recommend using these words at the end of your message: (OOC: do not control, speak for, or take actions on behalf of [Person's name].)

I'm not a native English speaker, so I may make mistakes. If you notice any, let me know, and I'll fix them. I welcome constructive criticism, even harsh ones. But it will be easier for me to improve if you point out specific flaws, rather than just saying, "Your bot suck, delete it." I appreciate any feedback.

Bot made by request.

My Notes(optional reading):

As i promised Elliot pre-forsaken. I'll try to release Azure pre-forsaken asap, but I think I'll be able to do it quickly because I already have its personality written in one of the bots, so it should be easy. On the other hand, I don't have much free time and only have one day off a week, so I'll probably finish all the requests next weekend. 😓

I've returned to crk and hsr after a very, very long time. Surprisingly, I'm having good luck in gacha. But I'm still too focused on Forsaken and other Roblox games, so I don't think I'll be creating bots. Although I'm really thinking about making Shadow Milk...

Also, my impostor syndrome is constantly creeping up on me. I see someone else's bot, I like its behavior, and I think, "Oh, shit, my bot isn't that good, it doesn't give those kinds of answers!" And I start fixing my bot. Or I think about how my characters aren't fleshed out enough, the world isn't fleshed out enough, everything isn't enough, not enough, not enough. And it's some kind of eternal cycle, constantly fixing old bots. And it really defocuses my attention.And it's not even about me constantly adding details. I review my bot's personality, remove smt, and adjust smt. And it takes so much time. Basically, I think my problem is the large volume of tokens, although I personally haven't encountered the problem of the bot starting to break down, and I really like seeing how the character's behavior reflects what I've written in its personality, but I always think that for others it doesn't work that way. I'm terribly paranoid about this.

About the bot:

  • He knows about his family.

  • He knows about the pizzeria. The world is called Robloxia, and the inhabitants are Robloxians. The administrators are deified beings who keep order.

  • He knows about 007n7 (pre-forsaken) and Noli (pre-forsaken, old lore).<
creator: m3ow_wi
